ety: perf. part. of farmūdan; see farmā
def: part. & s.m. Ordered, commanded; said; — that which is commanded, order, command, precept.
more: part. pass. (of فرمودن) Ordered, said; s. m. Order, command, precept.

ety: = farod, q.v.; — prob. akin to Zend aora, 'down' = S. अवर
def: prep. & adv. Down; below, beneath, under
more: adv. Down; below; under. faro k. , v. a. To suppress; quell.
adv. Down, below, under. فروهونا faro honā, To subside.
subs: v.t. To put or keep down, to restrain, subdue, suppress, quell
v.n. To go down, to subside; to become low, to be reduced.

ety: Pārsī frôt; Pehl. frôt; prob. fr. Zend frā or fra = S. प्र; cf. Zend pāreñtare
def: s.m. Descending, descent; alighting, stopping; — the arrival and deposit of goods within certain defined limits; — prep. & adv. Down, below, beneath ( = faro)
more: n. m. 1. ( In comp .) Alighting. 2. Arrival and deposit of goods within certain limits. farod-gāh , n. f. A halting or landing place; a camping ground.
s. m. Descending, alighting, stopping; adv. Down, beneath. فرودانا farod ānā, To descend, to come down.
subs: v.n. To come down, to descend, to alight
s.f. Place of alighting; halting-place, camping-ground.

def: n. f. 1. Omission. 2. Connivance. faro-guzāsht k. , v. a. 1. To omit; leave out. 2. To pass over; overlook. faro h. , v. n. To subside; to be reduced.
To omit, to except, to neglect, to fail.

ety: faro + guẕāsht, qq.v.
def: s.f. Passing over, omission; negligence, neglect, remissness, carelessness; overlooking, connivance; shortness, failing
subs: (-kī), To pass over, overlook, omit, except, leave; to neglect; to slignt; — to fall short (of), to fail.

ety: faro + kash, qq.v.
def: adj. (orig.) 'Pulling down'; alighting; staying, lodging, residing
subs: v.n. To alight; to stay lodge, reside.

ety: faro+mānda, qq.v.
def: adj. Broken, brought low, weak, feeble, powerless, helpless; fatigued, tired, weary; dejected, depressed; distressed; oppressed.
more: (comp. of فرو Low, under, and part. pass. of ماندن To live, to remain) adj. Dejected, tired, depressed, fatigued, weary, weak, helpless.

ety: faromānda, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Helplessness, weakness; dejection, despondency; distress, indigence, poverty.

ety: faro+mān, fr. māndan; see mānda+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. = faro-māndagī, q.v. s.f. Helplessness, weakness; dejection, despondency; distress, indigence, poverty.

ety: faro+māya, qq.v.
def: adj. Of low stock, ignoble, low, mean, abject, petty, base, worthless, sordid; humble, low; poor.
more: (comp. of فرو Below, under, and مايه Stock, capital, fund) adj. Worthless, poor, ignoble, abject, low, sordid, mean.

ety: faro+tan, qq.v.
def: adj. Depressed; submissive, lowly, humble.

ety: faro-tan, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Submissiveness, submission; lowliness, humility; condescension.
more: n. f. Humility; condescension. faro-kash h. , v. n. To alight, lodge, or put up at.

ety: prob. akin to S. स्फुर्
def: v.n. To snort or sneeze (a horse); to move briskly; to come quickly.
more: (perhaps, from s. r. स्फुर Start, throb. See فرّاٿا) v. n. 1. To sneeze (a horse). 2. (for پهـرانا) To come quickly.

ety: intens. n. fr. فرش; see firāsh
def: s.m. A servant whose business it is to spread carpets, make beds, light lamps, &c.; bed-maker; chamberlain; sweeper.
more: n. m. فرش spread. 1. A servant who spreads the carpets, prepares the lamps, etc. 2. A fir tree. farrāsh-khānā, toshe-khāna , n. m. The room where carpets, tents, etc. are kept.

(adj. superl. of فرش Heb. To spread) s. m. A servant whose business it is to spread the carpets, &c., bedmaker, chamberlain. ¶ firāsh, Spreading. فراشخانه firāsh-ḵẖāna, s. m. The room where carpets, &c. are kept.

ety: fr. farrāsh
def: s.f. Spreading carpets; — the business of a farrāsh
more: n. f. Spreading carpets, etc.; the business of a farrāsh. farrāshī paṅkhā , n. m. A large hand-pankhā.
s. f. Spreading carpets, &c. the business of a farrāsh. فراشي سلام farrāsh-ī-salām, A bow or obeisance made down to the carpet.
subs: (prob. corr. of firāshī or farāshī), adj. Of or relating to a carpet, &c.; — farrāshī pankhā, s.m. A large fan hung from the roof of a house; a large hand-fan (see pankhā)
s.m. A bow or obeisance made down to the carpet.

ety: prob. fr. S. स्फुर
def: s.m. The sound of anything rushing or fluttering in the air; — a rushing sound; a fluttering in the air; a rushing, rush; — a puff; — a running (over), or a rapid glance (through — a page, &c.)
more: (see پهـرانا pharrānā, from r. स्फुर Start, throb) s. m. The sound of any thing rushing or fluttering in the air, a rush, a puff.
subs: v.n. To rush or fly along, to run swiftly, to run a heat or race; to read fast.

def: n. m. A. فر fly. S. स्फुर throb. 1. A run; running over (a page). 2. A fluttering in the air; rustling; rush. Kyā farrāte kī havā chal rahī hai. farrāṭe lenā yā bharnā , v. n. 1. To run a race; to fly. 2. To read fast.

ety: i.e. far+ruḵẖ, q.v.
def: adj. 'Beautiful-faced'; happy, fortunate.

ety: farruḵẖ, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Happiness, prosperity.
prop. n. Persia; one of the southern provinces of Persia, the ancient Bactria.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَارِس (fāris), from Arabic فَارِس (fāris), from Early New Persian پَارْس (pārs, “Persia, Persis”).

ety: rt. & imperf. part. of farsūdan, far = Zend fra = S. प्र;+sūdan, rt. Zend śā = S. शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. Wearing, rubbing; obliterating, effacing; worn, obliterated, old (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jān-farsā, 'distracting, or wearing out, the mind').
more: part. pres. (of فرسودن) in compos. Wearing, rubbing, worn, obliterating, obliterated.

def: s.m. The arabicized form of the next, q.v.
more: s. m. A parasang (or measure of about 18,000 feet), a league.

ety: prob. far = Zend fra = S. प्र 'forward'+sang, 'stone,' q.v.
def: s.m. A parasang (about 3¾ English miles), a league.
more: s. m. A parasang (or measure of about 18,000 feet), a league.

ety: inf. n. of فرش 'to spread'
def: s.m. Spreading (a carpet, &c.); paving; — what is spread (of household furniture, &c.), carpeting, bedding; a carpet, a mattress, a bed; a mat; a floor-cloth; a pavement; — the floor, ground; a wide or spacious plain or place; the earth (e.g. ʻarsh-se-le farsh-tak, 'from the highest heaven to the earth')
more: n. m. 1. Floor; ground. 2. A pavement. 3. Any cloth spread out on the ground; a floor cloth; carpet. farsh par , adv. On the floor. farsh furūsh , n. m. The carpets, etc. of a house. farsh k. , v. a. 1. To spread a carpet. 2. To pave. 3. ( Slang ) To knock one down. Māre jūtoṅ ke farsh kar dūṅgā! Slang. farsh h. , v. n. 1. To be spread (a carpet, etc.). 2. To be paved. az farsh tā ba-arsh . From earth to heaven. patthar kā farsh . A stone pavement. pakkā farsh . A pakkā floor; a pavement. kachchā farsh . A kachcha floor. lab-i-farsh . The edge of the floor cloth, or carpet. lab-i-farsh tak tāzīm k. , v. a. To go the end of the carpet to receive one. mīr-farsh . A weight to keep down a floor cloth.

(Heb. To spread) s. m. 1. Spreading. 2. Carpetting, bedding, a cushion, mat, carpet, pavement, any thing spread. فرش کرنا farsh karnā, To spread, pave. فرش هونا farsh honā, To be spread. ¶ furush, s. plur. (of فراش firāsh ) Beds, carpets.
subs: v.t. To spread (a carpet); to pave; — to lay (one) flat, fell, knock down
v.n. To be spread; to be paved.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرش (farš), from Arabic فَرْش (farš, “carpet, rug”).

ety: rel. n. fr. farsh
def: adj. Of or relating to a carpet, or a floor; reaching down to the carpet or floor; — flat, even; flat bottomed; — s.f. A kind of ḥuqqa ( = farshī ḥuqqa, q.v.)
more: adj. 1. Belonging to a floor or carpet. 2. Flat bottomed. farshī , n. m. ( Slang ) A catamite. farshī jūtā , n. m. A slipper. farshī huqqā, farshī , n. m. A huqqā , with a flat bottom. farshī salām , n. m. A very low bow; obeisance; a protracted salām .
s. f. A kind of ḥuḳḳa.
subs: s.m. A large ḥuqqa with a flat bottom which stands on the ground (in contradistinction to a small one which is carried in the hand)
s.m. A bow or obeisance made down to the carpet or the floor, a very low bow.

ety: for orig. pārsī, rel. n. fr. pārs
def: adj. Persian; — s.f. The Persian language; — a foreign language, an unknown tongue; — the p language
more: n. f. 1. The Persian language. 2. A foreign language; High Dutch; the p language. Kanjaṛoṅ kī fārsī hī alag hai. fārsī baghārnā , v. a. To speak an unknown language; to speak High Dutch. Yeh apnī fārsī alag hī baghārte haiṅ. fārsī-dāṅ, yā khuāṅ , n. m. A Persian scholar. fārsī kī ṭāṅg toṛnā , v. a. To speak bad Persian.
1. s. f. Persian language. 2. adj. Persian. فارسي والا fārsī-wālā, A Persian scholar.
subs: (apnī), To speak a Persian of one's own; to speak an unknown language; to speak High Dutch
or fārsī-dān, s.m. A Persian scholar
(colloq.) To speak halting or bad Persian.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَارْسِی (fārsī), from Early New Persian پَارْسِی (pārsī), influenced by Arabic فَارِسِيّ (fārisiyy). Doublet of پارْسی (pārsī).

ety: abst. s. fr. fārsī
def: s.f. Persian language; Persian scholarship, knowledge of the Persian language.

ety: perf. part. of farsūdan; see farsā
def: part. Rubbed, worn; effaced, obliterated; destroyed, torn; spoiled by age, decayed; — bruised, crushed, wounded.
more: part. pass. (of فرسودن) Worn, torn, spoiled by age, obliterated.

def: (inf. n. of فرط 'to be foremost in attaining' (to), &c.), s.m.f.(?) Excess, extravagance, exorbitance; exceeding degree; superfluity; abundance; — mastery, ascendency, prevalence, predominance; — an erect way-mark, a guide-post; — a small hill; the top or summit of a hill.
adj. & s.m. Old and decrepit; good for nothing; — an old dotard.

ety: for tiryāq-ě-fārūq
def: s.m. The best sort of treacle or theriac (so called because it makes a distinction (farq) between disease and health).

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.f. February.
wety: Borrowed from English February, possibly influenced by Portuguese fevereiro. Compare Classical Persian فَبْرَوِی (fabrawī), Konkani फेवरेर (phevrer).

def: adj. 1. Cor. of forward. 2. Full gallop. farvaṭ uṛānā , v. n. 1. To slap; beat. 2. ( Slang ), To have sexual intercourse with. farvaṭ jānā , v. a. To go at full gallop. farvaṭ k. , v. a. To forward (a letter).

ety: Zend fra+aretha; S. प्रार्थ (प्रार्थना), + अर्थ्
def: s.f. Exclamation; lamentation; cry for help, or redress; complaint; charge; suit
more: n. f. 1. A complaint; suit; charge. 2. A cry for help, succour, etc. faryād-ras , n. m. A redresser of grievances; a just judge; an administrator of justice. faryād-rasī , n. f. Redress of grievances. faryād k. , v. a. 1. To complain; lay a suit against one. 2. See duhāī denā. faryād ko pahuṅchnā , v. a. To hear one's suit or complaint.

s. f. Complaint, exclamation, lamentation, crying out for assistance. فريادرس faryād-ras, A redresser of grievances. فرياد رسي faryād-ras-ī, s. f. Redress, succour. فرياد کرنا faryād karnā, To complain, cry out for assistance.
subs: adj. & s.m. Redressing complaints; — a redresser of grievances, a dispenser of justice; a helper, defender
s.f. Redress of grievances, redress, succour
v.n. To cry aloud for help or redress ( = duhāʼī denā, q.v.); to lament; to complain; to bring an action or suit (against, -kī)
(kisī-kī), To heed the cry for redress (of); to hear the complaint or suit (of), to relieve; to do justice (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَرْیَاد (faryād).

ety: faryād+ī = S. इन्
def: s.m. A seeker of redress or justice, a complainant, plaintiff.
a female given name, Faryal or Farial, from Persian, meaning “the one with a beautiful neck, good neck”
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فریال (faryāl).

ety: inf. n. of فرض 'to appoint' (to
def: adj. & s.m. Made obligatory or binding (by God); obligatory, incumbent; — a thing or duty made obligatory (by God, for neglecting which one will be punished), a divine command; a moral obligation, an indispensable duty; a statute, an ordinance, injunction, a command or prohibition (of the prophet Mohammad); obligation, onus, responsibility; — supposition, hypothesis, assumption
more: n. m. فرض made lawful. 1. Divine command; a duty, the omission of which is considered a mortal sin. 2. Duty; moral obligation; bounden duty. Khudā iske farz se adā kar de! Wom. 3. Onus ; responsibility; liability. 4. ( qarār-i-farzī ) Supposition; assumption; hypothesis; postulate. farz utārnā, yā adā k. , v. a. To discharge one's duty. farz paṛhnā , v. a. Mah. To read the farz or prayer to God. farz k. , v. a. 1. To impose a duty or obligation. 2. To assume; take for granted ( taslīm k .); put a case. farz karo ; A. bil-farz ; H. māno , Suppose; let; put the case that. farz kīyā yā mānā jāë . Let it be granted. farz-i-muhāl . An impossible hypothesis. Farz-i-muhāl muhāl nahīṅ . The impossible can be conceived. farz h. , v. n. To be incumbent on; devolve on; become a duty. ain farz . An absolute or imperative duty or obligation.

s. m. f. Divine command, a duty the omission of which is considered as a mortal sin, an obligation. فرض کرنا farẓ karnā, To consider as a positive indispensable duty, to consider as certain or unquestionable, to grant, admit, assume. فرض هونا farẓ honā, To be incumbent, to behoove.
subs: or farẓ adā karnā (-kā), To fulfil an obligation, discharge a duty
To repeat the enjoined prayers (to God)
v.t. To impose an obligation or a duty (on, -par); to consider as a positive indispensable duty; to regard as certain or unquestionable; to take for granted, to grant, admit, suppose, assume
s.m. An impossible or absurd hypothesis
(-ko or -par), To be obligatory (on), to be incumbent (on), to behove, to devolve (on), become (one's) duty
On the supposition or hypothesis (that); granted (that); suppose (cf. farẓ karo, and māno).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَرْض (farz), from Arabic فَرْض (farḍ). First attested in c. 1534 as Middle Hindi فرض (farz).

def: s.f. = firzīn, q.v.

ety: acc. of farẓ
def: adv. Supposing the case, on the supposition (that), hypothetically, ex hypothesi, for instance, for example; suppose.
more: adv. On the supposition; hypothetically; ex hypothesi .
adv. Hypothetically, for instance, suppose, granting.

ety: Pehl. farjānak; fr. Zend fra+rt. jan = S. प्र+ज्ञा
def: adj. & s.m. Learned, intelligent, wise; excellent, distinguished, noble, honourable; happy, blest; — a learned or wise man, &c.; — a pawn which has reached the last square on the board, and thereby become raised to the rank of farzīn, q.v.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Learning, science; wisdom; excellence.

ety: Pārsī frazañt; Pehl. fanzand; Zend frazaiñti, rt. zan with fra = S. प्र+जन्
def: s.m. A favourite son; a dutiful son
more: n. m. Z. pandand . A son; a child. Farzand woh jo pand māne, Aur bāp kā kahnā farz jāne . Gulzār Nasīm. A son is he who precepts will obey, to his father's bidding heed will pay. farzand-i-khalaf . A dutiful son. farzand-i-nā-khalaf . An undutiful son.
s. m. f. A child, son or daughter. فرزند رشيد farzand-i-rashīd, A good child.
subs: (-ko), To adopt as a son or daughter, &c. ( = farzandī-meṅ lānā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرزند (farzand), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *ǵenh₁-.

ety: farzand, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. The state or condition of a child; childhood; — the relation of a child to its parent, sonship, &c.
more: n. f. The relation of a son to a parent. farzandī meṅ lenā, farzand banānā, yā k. , 1. To adopt as a son. 2. To make one his son-in-law.
s. f. The state or condition of a child. فرزندي مين لينا farzaṉd-ī meṅ lenā, To adopt.
subs: (-ko), To adopt as a son or daughter; — to make (one) a son-in-law.

ety: rel. n. fr. farẓ
def: adj. Obligatory, binding, incumbent, imperative; — assumed, assumptive, hypothetical; supposititious, fictitious, not real or essential.
more: adj. 1. Incumbent; obligatory; bound; obliged; indispensable; imperative. 2. Supposititious; fictitious; not real or essential; nominal. 3. Assumed; hypothetical. farzī muqaddama, yā muāmala . A fictitious suit. ism-i-farzī , n. m. A fictitious or assumed name.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرضی (farzī), from Arabic فَرَضِيّ (faraḍiyy).

ety: farz, fr. Z. fra+jan = S. प्र+ज्ञा;+ī or īn = Zend aêna = S. इन् (ई)
def: s.m. (f.?) The queen (at chess; — prop. the vazīr or minister)
more: s. f. The queen at chess. فرزين بند farzīn-band, s. m. A term used at chess.
subs: (-ko), To play a pawn up to the rank of queen
adj. Guarded by the queen (a piece, at chess); — checkmating the queen (?).

def: promoting to fers (in shatranj)
n. m. The queen at chess. farzīṅ banānā , v. a. ( Chess ) To play a pawn up to the rank of the queen. farzīṅ-band , n. m. Checkmating the queen.

ety: farz, fr. Z. fra+jan = S. प्र+ज्ञा;+ī or īn = Zend aêna = S. इन् (ई)
def: s.m. (f.?) The queen (at chess; — prop. the vazīr or minister)
more: s. f. The queen at chess. فرزين بند farzīn-band, s. m. A term used at chess.
subs: (-ko), To play a pawn up to the rank of queen
adj. Guarded by the queen (a piece, at chess); — checkmating the queen (?).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَرْزِین (farzīn).

ety: for A. فرضية, farẓ+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. An indispensable precept; — hypothesis, assumption.
Fez (the capital city of the region of Fez-Meknes, Morocco)
wety: From Arabic فَاس (fās).

ety: inf. n. of فسد 'to be bad, evil, or corrupt,' &c.
def: s.m. Corruptness, unsoundness, wrongness, wickedness, viciousness, depravity, iniquity, dishonesty; — disorder, disturbance, mischief, violence, waste, ruin, horror, intrigue, sedition, mutiny, rebellion, war; disagreement, discord, dissension, strife, quarrelling, quarrel, brawl
more: n. m. فسد was corrupt. 1. Brawl; outbreak (دنگا 2). 2. Dissension; ferment. 3. Intrigue. fasād barpā k., uṭhānā, machānā, yā k. , v. a. See daṅgā k. fasād-i-khūn , n. m. Disease in the blood. fasād-i-azīm . Violent breach of the peace. fasād kī jaṛ , n. f. 1. Matter or cause of dispute. 2. A fomentor of disturbances ( āg-lagāū , 2). fasād kī nīyat se , adv. Corruptly; maliciously; with evil intent. fasād-i-mulk . Political disturbance. fasād-i-medah . Stomachic disease.
(from فسد) s. m. Depravity, iniquity, wickedness, mischief, violence, war, horrour, mutiny, sedition, rebellion.
subs: or fasād bar-pā karnā, or fasād maćānā, v.n. To create or excite a disturbance, &c.
s.m. Bad state of the blood
adv. With evil intent, corruptly, wickedly, maliciously.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَسَاد (fasād).

ety: rel. n. fr. fasād
def: adj. & s.m. Wicked, vicious, mischievous; factious, seditious, mutinous, rebellious, turbulent, quarrelsome, &c.; a vicious or mischievous person; a turbulent fellow, a quarrelsome person, a brawler.
more: adj. Quarrelsome, vicious, mutinous, rebellious, seditious, mischievous, &c.

ety: for A. فصاحة, inf. n. of فصح 'to be or become good or correct in language or speech'
def: s.f. Chasteness in language or speech; pureness, or perspicuousness, in speech or language; eloquence, fluency of speech
more: n. f. فصح was clear. Pure or good language; eloquence ( khush-bayānī ). Nasīm-i-Dehlvī, ham mūjid-i-bāb-i-fasāhat haiṅ; Koī Urdū ko kyā samjhegā jaisā ham samajhte haiṅ! Nasīm. Nasīm of Delhi, I am fount of language fine, Who Urdu knows as I, who copes with verse like mine? fasāhat se, fasāhat ke sāth , Eloquently.
(from فصح To speak clearly, from Heb. To break; and, hence, to sound) s. f. Eloquence.
subs: or faṣāḥat-ke-sāth, adv. With eloquence, eloquently.

ety: = afsān; rt. Zend śā with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+शा (शो); cf. S. शाण, and H. sān
def: s.f. A whetstone.
s.m. A tale, story, fiction, fable, romance; a narrative.
more: n. m. A romance ( afsānah ). Jo kuchh sunā tha woh thā fasanah, jo kuchh ki dekhā woh khuāb dekhā . All that I heard a fairy tale did seem, And all I saw flit by me as a dream.
s. m. Tale, fiction, &c. See افسانه afsāna.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فسانه (fasāna). Doublet of افسانہ (afsāna).

ety: inf. n. of فصد 'to cut, to open' (a vein
def: s.f. Opening a vein, bleeding; phlebotomy
more: n. f. Opening a vein; bleeding; phlebotomy. fasd khulnā , v. n. To be bled. fasd khulvānā , v. a. To cause to be bled. Kudāl se fasd khulvāo! (Fac.) fasd kholnā, yā lenā , v. a. To bleed; open a vein.
s. f. Opening a vein, bleeding, phlebotomy. فصد کرنا faṣd karnā or فصد کهـولنا faṣd kholnā, v. a. To bleed.
subs: (-kī), A vein to be opened, to be bled
(-kī), To cause a vein to be opened, to have or get (one) bled
or faṣd lenā (-kī), To open (one's) vein, to bleed (one).

ety: for orig. pāsh; prob. akin to S. स्पर्श् (स्पृश्)
def: adj. Spread abroad, divulged, known, apparent, manifest, notorious, palpable, gross
more: adj. See ظاﮩو and بهاري 19. Notorious ( praghaṭ ). fāsh galatī k. , v. a. To make a gross or serious mistake. fāsh k. , v. a. To lay bare or open; let out ( praghaṭ k .). fāsh h. , v. n. To be divulged or exposed; to get abroad.
adj. Apparent, manifest, known, divulged, notorious. فاش کرنا fāsh karnā, To divulge, reveal, discover. فاش هونا fāsh honā, To be divulged or revealed.
subs: v.t. To divalge, reveal, expose, lay bare

def: s.m.(?), Easter, the passover.

ety: fr. fashārdan = afshārdan; rt. Zend khshar with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+क्षर्
def: s.m. Squeezing, pressing (with the hand); compression, constriction; — a scattering; diffusion ( = fishānī).
more: s. m. Squeezing, pressing with the hand, compressing.

def: s.m. A species of ivy; — bryony.

ety: act. part. of فسد 'to be or become bad, or corrupt,' &c.
def: adj. Bad, evil, corrupt, unsound; unlawful, criminal, wrong, improper, wicked, vicious, unprincipled, depraved, dishonest; mischievous, pernicious, noxious, malignant
more: adj. فسد was corrupt. See برا 6—8, 12, and jhūṭā , 1, 3, 6. 1. Corrupt; malignant. Mavād-i-fāsid . 2. Bad; wicked; unprincipled. 3. Wrong; sinister; guilty; culpable; criminal; felonious. 4. Unlawful ( nā-jāiz ). fāsid taur se , adv. Corruptly. fāsid k. , v. a. 1. To pervert; corrupt; vitiate; debase ( kharāb k. , 3, 4). 2. To invalidate ( toṛnā , 13). nīyat yā ilm-i-fāsid . Criminal intention or knowledge.
m. (part. act. of فسد) adj. Vicious, perverse, depraved, corrupt, noxious, pernicious, bad. اخلاق فاسده aḵẖlāḳ-i-fāsida, Vicious or bad disposition or manners.
subs: v.t. To corrupt, pervert, debase, &c.; to vitiate invalidate.
wety: From Arabic فَاسِد (fāsid).

def: (for A. فاسدة fāsidat, fem. of fāsia), adj. f. = fāsid, q.v.
more: f. (part. act. of فسد) adj. Vicious, perverse, depraved, corrupt, noxious, pernicious, bad. اخلاق فاسده aḵẖlāḳ-i-fāsida, Vicious or bad disposition or manners.
subs: Bad or vicious disposition or manners.

ety: v.n. fr. فصح; see faṣāḥat
def: adj. Chaste, pure, or sweet (a word, or expression, or speech); perspicuous, clear; polished (language); eloquent, fluent (of speech).
more: (from فصح) adj. Eloquent, sweet (tongued). (Pers. plur. فصيحان).

ety: faṣīḥ, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Eloquence, &c. ( = faṣāḥat, q.v.). s.f. Chasteness in language or speech; pureness, or perspicuousness, in speech or language; eloquence, fluency of speech
subs: or faṣāḥat-ke-sāth, adv. With eloquence, eloquently.

ety: act. part. of فسق 'to depart from the right way,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Disobedient (to God), improus, unrighteous, sinful, wicked; vicious, immoral; — a transgressor, sinner; an immoral man; a worthless fellow; a fornicator, an adulterer (syn. fājir).
more: n. m. fāsiqa , n. f. فسق went astray. See فاجر
s. m. (part. act. of فسق Heb. To distend, to divaricate, to quit the path of rectitude) A fornicator, an adulterer, or adultress, a sinner, a worthless fellow. adj. Impious.

ety: for A. فاسقة fāsiqat, fem. of fāsiq
def: s.f. An adulteress, a harlot ( = fājira).
more: s. f. (part. act. of فسق Heb. To distend, to divaricate, to quit the path of rectitude) A fornicator, an adulterer, or adultress, a sinner, a worthless fellow. adj. Impious.

ety: v.n. fr. فصل; see faṣl
def: s.f. A low wall of enclosure in front of a fortress, or of the main wall of a city or town; a breastwork, intrenchment; rampart, wall of defence.
more: n. f. A rampart; city wall. fasīl bāhar . Outside the city wall.
(vulg. safīl, from فصل) s. f. Breastwork, parapet, intrenchment.

ety: for A. فاصلة, fem. of fāṣil, act. part. of فصل 'to separate,' &c.
def: s.m. Separating; separation; intervening space, interstice, interval; break, gap; space, distance
more: n. m. 1. Space; intermediate space; interval. 2. Break; gap; discontinuity; separation. 3. distance ( dūrī ). fāsile par, dūr , adv. At a distance; apart; far off; away from.
(from فصل) s. m. 1. Separating. 2. Space, intermediate space, interstice.
subs: adv. At a distance, far off; apart, away from.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فاصله (fāsila), from Arabic فَاصِلَة (fāṣila).

ety: inf. n. of فسخ 'to dislocate; to undo, or annul,' &c.
def: s.m. Dislocation; casting off, abandoning, giving up (an idea, &c.); undoing, dissolving, dissolution, annulling, annulment (of a contract, &c.); breaking, infraction, infringement, breach, violation (of an agreement, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Breach; infraction; infringement; violation. 2. Breaking or dissolution of a contract; annulment. faskh-i-izdivāj, faskh-i-nikāh . Annulment of a marriage. faskh k. , v. a. 1. To infringe ( toṛnā , 14). 2. To break a contract, etc. 3. To cancel; make void ( tardīd k . 2). faskh h. , v. n. To be rendered invalid; to be annulled.
s. m. Violation (as, of an agreement), infraction, dissolving (a marriage), dislocation. فسخ کرنا fasḵẖ karnā, To break off (a purpose, &c.), to break (an agreement or promise), to invalidate.
subs: v.t. To cast off, abandon, give up; to undo, dissolve, annul, cancel; to break, break off; to invalidate; to infringe; to make void
v.n. To be rendered invalid, to be annulled, &c.

ety: inf. n. of فصل 'to separate, or divide,' &c.
def: s.f. A separation, division, partition; one of the four divisions or seasons of the year, a season; season, time; reaping-season, harvest; crop or crops; a division, section (of a chapter, &c.); a chapter; an article; a clause; — distinguishing between what is true and what is false; decision, determination
more: n. f. فصل cut, separated. See sākh . 1. Section. 2. Season; the reaping season. Kharbūzoṅ kī fasl . 3. Crops. fasl-i-istādah ; H. khaṛī khetī . The standing crop. fasl-i-tukhm-rezī ; H. buār-belā . The sowing season; seed time. fasl-i-kharāb ; H. burī sākh , n. f. A deficient harvest. fasl-i-kharīf ; H. sāonī , n. f. The autumnal harvest of rice, millet, etc., requiring irrigation. The seed is sown at the commencement of the rainy season, and the crop is usually reaped after its close, or about Oct—Nov. The sub-divisions are Bhadai, Kuārī, Aghanī . fasl-i-rabī ; H. āsāṛhī , n. f. See rabī . This crop does not require irrigation or much water. The rabī includes the following crops—wheat, barley, pease, gram, poppy, masūr, sarsoṅ , linseed, tobacco, maṛvā, radhīā , cotton, arhar, jeṭhī dhān, kusum , mustard, birrā, sehuāṅ , and sugarcane. fasl kaṭne aur khaliyān se uṭhne-ke bād . After the…
s. f. 1. Separation, division, a section, article, clause, chapter. 2. Time, season, crop, harvest. (Plur. فصول fuṣūl ).
subs: s.f. The autumn; the autumnal harvest of rice, millet, &c. (the seed is sown at the commencement of the rainy season, and the crop is reaped about Oct.-Nov.)
s.f. The season of spring; the spring harvest (see rabīʻ; the crops of this season consist of wheat, barley, peas, gram, poppy, &c.)
s.f. The season of flowers, the spring ( = faṣl-ě-bahār or bahār).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَصْل (fasl), borrowed from Arabic فَصْل (faṣl). Cognate with Punjabi ਫ਼ਸਲ (fasal), Bengali ফসল (phośol), Assamese ফচল (phosol).

ety: rel. n. fr. faṣl
def: adj. Of or belonging to the season; of or relating to the harvest; — s.m. The revenue year ( = faṣlī sāl, q.v.)
more: adj. Belonging to the harvest, or season. faslī bukhār , The fever caused by change of the season. faslī bhaṛvā . An April fool ( holī kā bhaṛvā ). faslī sāl , n. m. The revenue or harvest year. The faslī year began on the 10th of the luṅar month Asin , corresponding to the 10th Sept., 1555. faslī mevah , The fruits of the season.
subs: s.m. Fever caused by change of season

s.m. The revenue or harvest year (the faṣlī era was instituted by the Emperor Akbar, and began on the 10th of Sept. (āsin) 1555; it then agreed with the Hijra; but as the faṣlī years are solar, it falls behind the latter era at the rate of three years per century; there are, however, different epochs of the revenue year in various parts of India)
s.m. The fruits of the season.

ety: intens. n. fr. fāṣid, act. part. of فصد 'to cut, slit, open' (a vein
def: s.m. A bleeder, phlebotomist a surgeon.
more: (adj. superl. of فصد) s. m. A surgeon, phlebotomist.

ety: faṣṣād, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Bleeding, phlebotomy; surgery.

ety: inf. n. of فتي 'to be youthful,' &c.
def: s.m. Youth; — (for A. فتي fataṉ) a youth, a young man.
more: (from فتي To be young: Heb. To be simple, easily seduced or persuaded) s. m. A youth.

def: s.m. A youth, a young man ( = فتا fatā, q.v.). s.m. Youth; — (for A. فتي fataṉ) a youth, a young man.
immediately, quickly, swiftly, speedily, in no time
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi فَٹافَٹ (faṭāfaṭ) / ਫਟਾਫਟ (phaṭāphaṭ). Phonetic changes of /pʰ/ to /f/ usually do not occur in Urdu (unlike Hindi and Punjabi) onomatopoeic. Related to پھٹ (phaṭ, “bang”).

ety: for A. فطانة, inf. n. of فطن 'to be intelligent,' &c.
def: s.f. Intelligence, intellect, understanding, wisdom; perception, discernment, sagacity, shrewdness, skill.

ety: inf. n. of فتح 'to open,' &c.
def: s.f. An opening, aperture; a beginning, start; — victory, conquest, triumph
more: n. f. فتح opened. Pl. fatūhāt . Victory; success (جے). 1. Fatah aur shikast Khudā ke hāth hai . Prov. Victory and defeat in God's hands are. Prov. 2. Fatah Khudā ke hāth hai, mār mār to kīye jāo . Prov. Victory is from God, but strike out all you can. fatah pānā , v. n. To get or obtain a victory. fatah-pech , n. m. 1. A mode of tying the turban. 2. A kind of huqqā snake. fateh khāṅ . A brave man. Mare na chūhī, aur nām Fateh Khāṅ! Prov. He never killed so much as a rat, though they call him Lord Conqueror. fateh khāṅ kā sālā . See Rustam kā sālā. fatah kā naqqārā, yā ḍankā , n. m. The beat or flourish of drums after a victory. fatah k. , v. a. 1. To conquer (جيتَنا). 2. To set out ( sidh k . 4). fatah-gaḍh ; H. jaë-thamb , n. f. A pillar or monument to commemorate a victory. fatah-nāma , n. m. Despatches announcing a victory; a bulletin. fatah h. , v. n. To be conquered. fateh-yāb, fate…

(Heb. To open) s. f. 1. An aperture, opening. 2. Victory, conquest. فتح پيچ faṭh-pech, A mode of tying the turband. فتح مند fatḥ-mand, adj. Victorious, conqueror, triumphant. فتح مندي fatḥ-mand-ī, s. f. Victory, victoriousness. فتح نامه fatḥ-nāma, s. m. Despatches announcing victory. فتح نشان fatḥ-nishān, A victorious standard (opened only in battle). فتح نصيب fatḥ-naṣīb, adj. Victorious, of victorious fortune. فتح ياب fatḥ-yāb, adj. Victorious. فتح کرنا fatḥ karnā, To conquer. فتح هونا fatḥ honā, To be conquered.
subs: s.m. A mode of tying the turban; — a kind of ḥuqqa-snake
v.n. To make a beginning, to enter (on); to start, to set out; — v.t. To conquer: fatḥ-gaḍh, s.f. A pillar or monument to commemorate a victory
adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant; victor, conqueror
s.m. Despatches announcing a victory, a bulletin
s.m. A victorious standard (opened only in battle)
adj. Of victorious fortune or destiny; victorious
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَتْح (fath), from Arabic فَتْح (fatḥ).

ety: for A. فتحة fatḥat, fr. fatḥ, q.v.
def: s.m. The sign (َ) of the vowel-sound a (pronounced as the English a in cedar; — syn. zabar).
more: n. m. See zabar , n. m. The vowel point (ُ) pronounced as the English a in the word America or French e in le , or Sanskrit अ.
s. m. The vowel pronounced as the English a in the word America, or the French e in le.

def: Fatehpur
wety: From فَتْح (fath, “victory”) + ـپُور (-pūr, “city or town”), literally “city or town of victory”.

def: Fatehpur Sikri (a town in India)

ety: act. part. of فتح 'to open,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Opening; conquering; — opener; conqueror.

ety: for A. فاتحة fātiḥat, fr. fātiḥ, q.v.
def: s.f. Commencement, first part (of a thing); exordium; — the opening chapter, or exordium, of the Qorʼān (so called because the recitation in prayer is commenced therewith); prayers for the dead; prayers offered up in the name of saints, &c.; oblations and offerings to saints, &c.
more: n. f. فتح to open. 1. The opening Sūrat or chapter of the Quran which, being read for the benefit of dying Mahomedans the word has come, in India, to signify prayers offered up for the deceased on different days after their death, accompanied by alms and distribution of food. 2. Oblations and offerings to saints, with various prayers and ceremonies at marriages and funerals, including one observed by women in commemoration of Fātima . fātihā paṛhnā , v. n. 1. To read prayers over the dead. 2. To be despondent or disappointed. Māl kī ab fātihā paṛho! fāteha-khair , n. f. 1. W. Mah. marriage. 2. Blessings on the dead. fāteha denā , v. a. To make offerings to saints with prayers.

(from فتح) 1. s. m. Commencement, exordium. 2. s. f. The first chapter or سوره of the ḳurʼān, which being repeated when praying for the souls of the dead, the word comes to mean prayers offered up in the name of saints, &c. and vulgarly oblations made to saints, &c.
s. Prayers or offerings for the dead. نيت خير کي فاتحه nīyat ḵẖair kī fātiḥa (dakh.) Fātiḥa offered in the name of the living ( Qanoon-e-islam, glossary).
subs: (-kī), To repeat the fātiḥa (over), to repeat prayers for the dead; — to give (a thing) up as lost (e.g. māl-kī ab fātiḥa paṛho); to be disappointed or despondent
s.f. A prayer for good or for blessings; a marriage-prayer; marriage; blessings on the dead

(-ke nām), To cause blessings to be invoked (upon a deceased relative; this is done by having the opening chapter of the Qorʼān read over the deceased for forty days after his death, and distributing alms and food)
(-ke nām), To invoke blessings (on a deceased relative; see fātiḥa dilānā); to make offerings to saints with prayers.

ety: for A. فتيلة faṭīlat, fr. fatīl, 'twisted,' v.n. fr. فتل 'to twist'
def: s.m. A wick; a match; a fuse (see falīta)
more: (from فتل Heb. To twist a rope) s. m. 1. A match, a wick. فتيله سوز fatīla-soz, s. m. A candlestick.
subs: or fatīl-soz, s.m. A candlestick; a brass or tin lamp.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فتیله, from Arabic فَتِيلَة (fatīla).

def: n. m. A brass or tin lamp.

ety: for A. فاطمة fāt̤imat
def: s.f. The daughter of the prophet Mohammad, and the wife of the Caliph ʻAlī.
more: s. f. Name of Muhammadʼs daughter and wife of Ali.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. فطير 'unleavened dough,' fr. فطر; see fat̤r
def: adj. Unleavened; — s.f. Unleavened bread (opp. of ḵẖamīrī).
more: n. f. Opp. of khamīrī . Unleavened bread.

ety: inf. n. of فتق 'to slit, rend, &c.
def: s.m. A rupture, hernia; — the scrotum (?).
more: n. m. فتق rent. 1. Scrotum; testicles. Qasd Baṅgāle kā kar dījiye faskh, aë sāhib! Farz kardam ki wahāṅ jā-ke arākīn hue, Toṅbe do nikleṅge, ek halq se, ek fitaq se, Phir to, sāhab, na rahe āp, goyā bīn hue! All thought of going to Bengal forego! Grant you 're a minister of state raised to, Two gourds upon your throat and scrotum grown, You 're not yourself but a sitār outblown. 2. A rupture; hernia.
(from فتق To cleave, to split, to open) s. m. A rupture or hernia.

def: (v.n. fr. فتق 'to slit,' &c.), adj. Patentiore rima (mulier).

ety: inf. n. of فطر 'to cleave, split; to create, &c.
def: s.m. Cleaving, splitting, rending, breaking, &c.; creating; producing; originating, commencing.
more: (Heb. and Chald. To break, to split) s. m. Splitting, creating, commencing. ¶ fit̤r, Breaking a fast; the festival held on breaking Lent, which is also called عيد الفطر.

ety: intens. n. fr. فتن 'to burn'; 'to test by fire,' &c.
def: s.m. A goldsmith; an assayer of gold; — a great tempter; the Devil; — adj. Tempting, seductive; mischievous, malicious, seditious (cf. fitna).
more: 1. adj. (superl. from فتن) Seditious, mischievous, malicious. 2. s. m. An assayer (of gold).

ety: prob. fr. A. fatūḥ, fr. fatḥ, 'opening'
def: s.f. A kind of jacket or vest without sleeves, a short waistcoat.
more: n. f. A jacket without sleeves.
(q. d. open; from فتح) s. f. A kind of jacket without sleeves.

def: To give judgment, to decree.

ety: v.n. fr. افتي afta, 'he notified the decision of the law,' iv of فتو
def: s.m. A notification of the decision of the (Muhammadan) law in or respecting a particular case; a judicial decree, judgment, sentence, award, decision
more: n. m. The written verdict of the Mah. Law officer of a court, according to the Shara . fatvā denā , v. a. To give a verdict. Ishq ke muftī ne yūṅ fatvā dīyā, Dekhnā khūbāṅ kā dars-i-khūb hai . Walī. Love's master of the Rolls his verdict gives 'Love's veriest lore in lovely features lives.' fatvā lenā , Mah., v. a. To take a legal opinion of the lawfulness of an act.
(from فتو) s. m. A judicial decree, judgment, sentence, award, decision.
subs: To notify the decision of the law (in or respecting a case); to give a verdict, give judgment, to decree
(-meṅ), To seek or demand (of a lawyer) a notification of the decision of the law (in or respecting a question); to take a legal opinion (in respect of the lawfulness of an act, &c.).

def: s.m. (?) The betel-nut, Areca catechu.
s.f. A crowd (of men, &c.), a multitude, a host, an army
more: n. f. Army ( senā ). fauj-i-bahrī , n. f. Navy; fleet; armada; men-of-war; line of battle-ships. fauj-i-barrī , n. f. Land forces; military force; the army; militia; troops. fauj bhartī k. , v. a. To enlist (recruits).
(from افاجة To hasten, to run) s. f. An army, a multitude. (Plur. افواج). فوجکشي fauj-kash-ī, s. f. The collecting or levying of an army.
subs: s.f. A fleet, navy (opp. to fauj-ě-barrī, 'land forces')
To enlist recruits, to fill up an army
s.m. A (criminal) judge or magistrate (under the Muhammadan government); a superintendent of the military force in a district; an officer of the police so called

adj. Of or relating to a fauj-dār; — of or relating to crime, (a court) for criminal cases; — s.f. The office or jurisdiction of a fauj-dār or magistrate; — the criminal court; — a criminal case; fighting, assault, breach of the peace, &c.
or ʻadālat-ě-fauj-dārī, s.f. The (subordinate) criminal court (formerly the niz̤āmat ʻadālat); — fauj-dārī sipurd karnā (-ko), To send (a case) to the criminal court
To commit a criminal offence; to commit an assault, or a breach of the peace, &c.
s.f. The troops of a fauj-dār; provincial troops or militia (employed in garrisoning forts, and escorting treasure, and in revenue and police duties)
s.f. The collecting or levying of an army; the marching or advancing (against, -par); — incursion, inroad.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَوْج (fawj), borrowed from Arabic فَوْج (fawj), from فَيْج (fayj), borrowed from Middle Persian pdk'. First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi فوج (fvj /⁠fauj⁠/).

def: n. m. Under the Mah. Government, a criminal judge or magistrate; a superintendent of the military force in a district.
more: An officer of the police so called, a magistrate.

def: n. f. 1. The office of a magistrate; the criminal court. 2. A criminal case. fauj-dārī adālat, adalat-i-fauj-dārī , n. f. The subordinate criminal court, formerly the Nizāmat adālat . faujdārī supurd k . To send a case to the Criminal court. fauj-dārī k. , v. a. To commit an assault or criminal offence. faujdārī karne ko mustaid honā , v. n. To threaten, or be about to commit a breach of the peace. fauj-dārī meṅ mākhūz k. , v. a. To prosecute criminally; to accuse or charge with a crime or misdemeanor. fauj-i-sehbandī , n. f. The troops of the Faujdār ; provincial troops or militia employed in garrisoning forts and escorting treasure, and in revenue and police duties. fauj-kashī , n. f. 1. Collecting or levying an army. 2. Inroad ( tākht ). fauj-kashī k .; H. fauj chaṛānā , v. a. To march or advance against; make an incursion. fauj meṅ bhartī h. , v. n. To be enlisted.
more: s. f. Office of a فوجدار faujdār.

ety: rel. n. fr. fauj
def: adj. Of or belonging to the army, military.
more: adj. Military. faujī hukkām . Military officers.
wety: From فوج (foj, “army”) + ـِی (-ī).

ety: primarily an adv. n. signifying 'the location that is above'; whence is derived the verb فوق, inf. n. fauq, 'to be superior to,' &c.
def: prep. & adv. Above, upon, over; — s.m. Superiority, excelling, excellence; pre-eminence; — eminence, altitude, loftiness; top, summit, extreme or highest point; culminating point; zenith
more: n. m. 1. Loftiness; summit; culminating point; zenith (اونچائي 1). 2. ( fauqīyat ) Pre-eminence (سبقت). 3. Preference ( tarjīh ). fauq-ul-bhaṛak , A. H. Glittering ( bhaṛkīlā , 1). fauq le jānā , To excel ( sabqat le jānā ). fauqīyat chāhnā , v. n. To strive for excellence or superiority; to be ambitious or emulous ( barābarī k .). fauqīyat hāsil h., yā pānā , v. n. To bear the palm.
s. m. Superiority, excellence, altitude, loftiness. prep. Above.

ety: rel.n. fr. fauq
def: adj. Superior; upper; — dotted above (as the letter ت, &c.).
more: adj. Upper; dotted above, as ت etc. zāviye-i-fauqānī ; H. ūprīkon . Angles at the base.

ety: for A. فوقية, abst. s. fr. fauqī, rel. n. fr. fauq
def: s.f. Superiority, excellence; pre-eminence; supremacy; preference
more: s. f. Excellence, preference, superiority, preeminence, supremacy. See فوق fauḳ.
subs: or fauqīyat ḥāṣil karnā (-par), To obtain, or acquire, superiority, &c. (over); to excel, surpass
(-par), To possess superiority or excellence (over), to be superior (to), to surpass, excel
(-se), To bear away the palm (from), to excel, surpass.

def: oblique plural of فَولاد (faulād)
vocative plural of فَولاد (faulād)

ety: inf. n. of فور 'to boil,' &c.
def: s.m. Haste, expedition, celerity
more: s. m. Celerity, haste. في الفور fi-l-faur, adv. Immediately, instantly.

ety: acc. of faur
def: adv. Quickly, immediately, directly, instantly, at once, suddenly.
more: adv. At once; promptly; straight way (ابهي 2).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فورا (fawran), from Arabic فَوْرًا (fawran).

def: immediate, urgent, prompt, rapid, instantaneous, instant
wety: Borrowed from Arabic فوري (fawriyy)

def: immediately, instantly, urgently, promptly, rapidly
breaking news

ety: inf. n. of فوت 'to pass away,' &c.
def: s.f. Passing away, escaping, being lost, failing; — escape; loss; failure; — death
more: n. f. Rare . Death (انتقال 5) faut-bilā vasīyat . Intestacy. faut h. , v. n. 1. To die; depart this life. 2. To be lost. Matlab faut ho gayā .
s. f. Death, failure, omission. فوت هونا faut honā, 1. To die. 2. To be interred.
subs: v.n. To pass away, escape, be lost; to fail; to die; to be interred.

ety: rel. n. fr. faut
def: adj. Passed away, dead, deceased; — (property) of one who dies intestate and without legal heirs (which escheats to the sovereign)
more: adj. Deceased; dead. fautī farārī . 1. Dead or absconded (persons). 2. A list of cultivators who have died, or have deserted their cultivations or homesteads. fautī-nāma , n. m. 1. A document reporting the death of the incumbent and the name of the heir; a certificate of death. 2. A list of the killed. naqsha-i-fautī , n. An obituary.

adj. 1. Dead. 2. (Property) of one who dies intestate and without legal heirs (which escheats to the sovereign). فوتي فراري faut-ī firār-ī, adj. 1. Killed and missing. 2. (Property) of persons dying intestate or absconding. فوتي نامه faut-ī-nāma, A list of the killed.
subs: adj. & s.m. Dead or absconded; killed and missing; of or belonging to persons who have died intestate or have absconded; — a list of cultivators who are either dead or have deserted their cultivations or homesteads
s.m. A list of the killed, or the dead; — a document reporting the death of an incumbent and the name of his heir or heirs; a certificate of death.

ety: a bar. form. fr. fautīda, as perf. part. of faut-īdan, 'to die,' fr. A. faut
def: s.f. Death, decease.
demise; funeral
wety: From فوت (faut, “passing away”) + ـگی (-gī).

def: (inf. n. of فوز 'to attain, or to win, good fortune,' &c.), s.m.(?) Fruition, success; gain; victory; salvation; escape, safety.
s. m. plur. of فايده q. v.
s.m. pl. (of fāʼida, q.v.), Benefits, advantages, gains, profits, &c.
s.m. pl. (of fākiha, for fākihat; rt. فکه 'to be cheerful,' &c.), Fruits; kinds of fruit.
more: s. m. plur. (of فاکـﮩ‍ه from فکه) Fruits.

ety: for A. فوارة fawwārat, fem. of فوار fawwār, intens. n. fr. فور 'to boil; to gush, well forth'
def: s.m. A fountain, a jet-dʼeau; a spring; a drain
more: n. m. A fountain (H. phuārā ). Ek charan ākās ko, tīn charan pātāl. Nā garje, nā bījre, barse mūsal-dhār . Riddle. Ans. A fountain. One foot upon the sky, three feet on worlds below, Nor thunder, lightning, but of rain a ceaseless flow. favvāra chhūṭnā , v. n. To burst or spout forth (a jet d' eau ). Khūn ke favvāre chhūt gaë .
(adj. superl. from فور To boil, like a pot or fountain. Analog. to Heb. To break) s. m. A fountain, a jet, drain, a spring. فواره چهـوٿنا fauwāra chhūṭnā, To burst or spout forth a jet dʼeau.
subs: (-kā), A jet-dʼeau to burst or spout forth, a fountain to play; a spring to gush or well forth.

ety: inf. n. of فيض; see faiẓ
def: s.m. Overflowing, overflow, abundance, redundance.
more: (from فيض) s. m. Abundance, overflow, redundance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَیضَان (fayzān), from Arabic فَيْضَان (fayḍān). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi فیضان (fyẓan).

ety: inf. n. of فضو 'to be wide or spacious,' &c.
def: s.f. Width, spaciousness, openness, extensiveness (of ground, &c.); an open area, a court, a yard; a spacious tract, a wide expanse of land, a plain (syn. maidān).
more: n. f. فضو was wide. See bahār , (2, 3). Openness; an open place; a plain.
s. f. Openness and extensiveness of ground or place, area.
ex: کُھلی فَضا میں چَہَل قَدْمی کَریں۔
khulī fazā mẽ cahal qadmī karẽ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَضَا (fazā), from Arabic فَضَاء (faḍāʔ)

def: s.m. = pā-zahr, q.v. s.m. Antidote, Bezoar stone.
s. plur. (of فضيلت) Virtues, excellences, learning.
s.f. pl. (of faẓīlat, q.v.), Excellences, excellent qualities, virtues; literary attainments, learning; sciences.
s.m. pl. (of faẓla, q.v.), Leavings, orts, &c.
terrible, horrible
wety: From Arabic فَظِيع (faẓīʕ).

ety: v.n. fr. فضح; see faẓīḥat
def: adj. Disgraceful, ignominious, infamous.

ety: for A. فضيحة, v.n. fr. فضح 'to disgrace,' &c.
def: s.f. Disgrace, shame, ignominy, infamy; — any disgraceful or shameful thing; a vice, fault; stigma, stain; — exposing the vices or faults of; abusing; quarrelling, wrangling (this word, which is one of very common occurrence, is corrupted by the uneducated to fajītī, fajītā, phajīta, fadīyat, phadīyat, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Shame; disgrace; infamy; ignominy; stain ( badnāmī ). Āp ko fazīhat, gair ko nasīhat . Prov. In you a vice, for others advice. 2. (Wom. fazītī ) Quarrel; wrangle; brawl. 1. ' Hāë Fazītī! hāë Fazītī!' Ans. ' Ham tum jīte haiṅ to bahuterī fazītiyāṅ ho raheṅgī .' 'Alas! Fazihat! 'Alas these constant bickerings! alas!' 'While we live we will have many more Fazīhats (bickerings).' (Said by a weaver to his wife on the death of their daughter Fazīhat). 2. Tū tiryā bad-kār harāmaṅṛ roj phajītā kartī, Terā pāp kaṭā nahīṅ yahāṅ se, jab āūṅ jab laṛtī . Play. fazīhat k .; Wom. fazītā k. , v. a. 1. See bad-nām k. , 2. To quarrel. fazīhat h .; Wom. fazītā h. , v. n. See rusvā h . To be in every body's mouth; to be the town talk.
(from فضح) s. f. Disgrace, ignominy, infamy. فضيحت کرنا faẓīḥat karnā, To disgrace, to defame. فضيحت هونا faẓīḥat honā, To be disgraced, to become infamous.
subs: (-kī), To disgrace, put to shame; to expose the faults or vices (of), to defame, to render infamous; — to wrangle, quarrel
v.n. To be disgraced, be exposed to shame or infamy, become infamous (syn. ruswā honā).

ety: rel. n. fr. faẓīḥat
def: adj. Shameful, disgraceful, ignominious, infamous; — s.f. (vulg. corr. fazītī, fajīhatī, phajīhatī, &c.), Disgrace, &c. ( = faẓīḥat, q.v.).
more: 1. adj. Disgraceful, infamous. 2. s. (dakh.) Disgrace, ignominy.

ety: act. part. of فضل 'to exceed'; 'to excel,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Exceeding, being over and above, abundant, in excess, redundant, superfluous, supernumerary; — excellent; virtuous; excellent in learning, learned, erudite; perfect, accomplished; — excess, redundance, superfluity; residue, remainder, surplus, balance; receipt in excess (of revenue, &c.); waste; — a learned man, an erudite person, a perfect or accomplished scholar
more: adj. فضل exceeded. 1. Superfluous; supernumerary; over and above; more than enough; running over; satis superque ( adhik , 1). 2. Accomplished; proficient; perfect; finished. fāzil , n. m. pl. fāzilāt . 1. Surplus; excess; remainder; balance. 2. Receipt in excess of revenue, etc. 3. Waste; residue; superfluity. 4. (pl. fuzalā ) An accomplished person; a scholar. fāzil-i-ajal , n. m. An accomplished scholar. fāzil-bāqī , n. f. Balance in favour. fāzil bāqī nikālnā , v. a. To strike the balance. fāzil nikalnā yā h. , v. n. To exceed amount at credit. fāzil vāsilāt, fāzil vusūl . Surplus receipt; extra or additional collections. fāzil h. , v. n. To be proficient, perfect, or accomplished.
(part. act. of فضل) adj. 1. Excellent, learned, virtuous. 2. Abundant.
subs: s.f. A balance in favour; a surplus
v.n. To exceed (amount at credit, &c.), to prove in excess; to excel; to become learned, &c.
v.n. To turn out or become learned; to be or become proficient, or perfect, or accomplished.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic فاضِل (fāḍil).

def: s.m. pl. (of fāẓil), Redundancies, superfluities; remainders, balances, &c.

ety: for A. فضيلة, v.n. fr. فضل; see faẓl
def: s.f. An excellent quality, excellence; virtue; perfection; eminence, greatness; superior excellence, superiority; superior attainments or knowledge, learning, proficiency
more: n. f. 1. See baṛāī , 4-6. 2. Preference ( tarjīh ). 3. Learning; proficiency. fazīlat kā tamgā, fazīlat kī pagṛī . A robe of honor; a diploma. fazīlat kī pagṛī baṅdhnā , v. n. To be conferred (a robe of honor).
(from فضل) s. f. Excellence, perfection, virtue, knowledge, learning.
subs: (-par), To be superior (to), to excel, surpass.

ety: inf. n. of فضل 'to exceed; to become redundant; to surpass or excel,' &c.
def: s.m. Excess, superabundance, exuberance; increase, gain; superiority, excellence; virtue; superior learning or knowledge, learning, wisdom; — a free gift, gratuity; grace, bounty; favour; benefit
more: n. m. 1. Bounty; grace. Khudā apnā fazl kare! 2. A dreadful thing; bugaboo. Mah. Wom. Chup! nahīṅ Allāh mīyāṅ kā fazal ā jāëgā . Mah. Wom. fazl-i-Ilahī , intj. Thank Heaven! God be praised! Hāth pāoṅ dīyā salāī, bāt karne ko—fazl-i-Ilāhī! Prov. His arms and legs as matches thin, his tongue, good God! how it doth run! fazl k . To shew mercy; to be bountiful. fazl-i-maulā, fazl rahe! intj. Mendicants. God be with you! fazl hai! All is well.
s. m. 1. Excellence, virtue. 2. Increase, gain. 3. Gift, reward, favour, grace.
subs: The grace of God; by the grace of God (all is well; or I am quite well, &c.); thank God! God be praised!
(-par), To be gracious (to); to show mercy or favour (to); to deal bountifully (with)
or faẓl-ě-maulā, The grace of God (be with you); God be with you (a form of benediction used by mendicants, &c.)
All is well (by the grace of God).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَضْل (fazl), borrowed from Arabic فَضْل (faḍl). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi فضل (fẓl /⁠fazl⁠/).

ety: for A. فضلة faẓlat, v.n. fr. فضل 'to exceed,' &c.; see faẓl
def: s.m. Remaining portion, remainder, residue; redundant portion, redundance, superfluity, exuberance; overgrowth; — leaving, refuse, orts; excrement; filth, dirt
more: n. m. 1. Leavings; refuse. 2. Filth; dirt. 3. ( Med .) Residue ( phok ).
s. m. 1. Remainder, redundance. 2. Leaving, refuse, offal.
subs: s.m. & f. An eater of leavings.

def: oblique plural of فَضْل (fazl)
vocative plural of فَضْل (fazl)
Fezzan (a region of Libya)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic فَزَّان (fazzān), from Latin Phazania.

def: the twenty-sixth letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twentieth of the Arabic, and twenty-third of the Persian alphabet). Its sound is that of the English f. It has no exact correspondent in Sanskrit, nor in any of the modern Aryan languages of India; in words introduced into these from the Persian or Arabic, फ ph is generally substituted for fe. As a numeral fe denotes 80.
more: the twentieth letter of the Arabic and twenty-third of the Persian alphabet, has no exact correspondent in Sanskrit, though in practice it is denoted by फ . Its sound is that of the English f. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 80. It is a radical letter. It is occasionally interchanged with پ as سپيد or سفيد White, پيل or فيل An elephant. Sometimes it is changed into و as وام for فام A colour. It admits of being substituted for ب as زفان for زبان A tongue, فااو for بااو With him; and sometimes for ث as جدف for جدث A tomb. In Arabic, moved or sounded with the vowel fatʼḥa, it is an inseparable particle, signifying and, therefore, then, so that.

def: s.m. pl. (of فعل fěʻl), Actions, deeds, works, &c.
more: s. plur. (of فعل) Actions, deeds, works.

ety: prob. fr. Zend pairiṅharsta, perf. part. of pairi-harez, 'to pick out,' &c. = S. परि+सर्ज (सृज्)
def: s.f. A list, catalogue; an index, a table of contents; an inventory, a schedule.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فهرست (fihrist). Compare Sindhi فهرست (fihrista).

def: n. f. A table; index ( fard ). fehrist-i-dehāt , n. f. A list of the villages of an estate or district. fehrist-i-rishteh-dārī . Kindred roll. fehrist-i-ām . A general list; file of suits. fehrist-i-fautī farārī , A list of dead and deserted. fehrist-i-kāgzāt . A list of papers. fehrist-i-mardum-shumārī , n. f. A census; poll. fehrist-i-mazāmīn ; H. sūchīpatr , Table of contents. fehrist meṅ nām chaṛhānā, dākhil karnā, yā likhnā , v. a. To insert in a register, etc. enroll; register; enlist.
more: s. f. An inventory, list, index, table of contents, &c.

oblique plural of فہرست
vocative plural of فہرست

ety: fr. faʻl, inf. n. of فعل 'to do,' &c.
def: s.m. A deed, an action, act; labour, work, operation; — acting, pretending, feigning, pretence, pretext, sham; — a shameful deed; an unnatural act; coition; adultery; — (in Gram.) a verb
more: n. m. 1. Act; action; deed; work; operation. 2. (Pop. fail ) Feigning; pretence; pretext; sham. 3. An unnatural act; adultery. 4. Carnal intercourse. 5. ( Gram .) The verb (H. kiryā ). fel-i-jāiz , n. m. A lawful act. fel-i-shanīā , n. m. Evil deeds; prostitution; adultery; unnatural offence. fel-zāminī , n. f. Security for good conduct. fel-i-abas , n. m. Lost labor. fel kā fāil . The agent. fel karānā . To submit to improper sexual intercourse, sodomy, etc. fel k. , v. a. 1. To do an act. 2. To have sexual intercourse with. 3. To be exacting. 4. ( fail bharnā, yā k .) To pretend ( bahānā k .). Fail kiyoṅ kar rahā hai? chal, haṭ bhī! Wom. Shauq. fel-i-lāzmī ; H. akarmak kiryā , A neuter verb. fail lānā , Pop. v. a. To dispute; quarrel. fel-mutāadī ; H. sakarmak kiryā . An active verb. fel-i-majhūl ; H. sahtā batāū kiryā . A passive verb. fail machānā , v. a. 1. To dissemble; play the hy…

(Heb. ) s. m. 1. Action, work, operation. 2. A verb. فعل عبث fiﻌl-i-aﻌbas̤, Lost labour, an absurd undertaking. فعل لازمي fiﻌl-i-lāzimī, A verb neuter. فعل متعدي fiﻌl-i-mutaʼaﻌddī, A verb active, transitive, or causal.
subs: s.f. A complete or attributive verb
s.f. An evil or shameful deed; adultery; prostitution; an unnatural offence
v.n. To do or perform an act; to have carnal intercourse (with); — to be exacting; — to pretend, sham
v.n. (colloq.), To pretend, dissemble, play the hypocrite; — to make a disturbance or row; — to be persistent, to insist
An incomplete or non-attributive verb; a defective verb
adv. Actually, in fact, in reality; presently, at the present time ( = fiʼl-ḥāl).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِعْل (fi'l), from Arabic فِعْل (fiʕl).

ety: acc. of fěʻl
def: adv. In fact, indeed; practically
more: adv. 1. Practically. 2. In fact; indeed. felan o qaulan , In word and deed. failī, failīhāyā ; Wom. n. m. A pretender; an impostor; a knave. failyā-pan , n. m. Knavery; villany.

wety: Borrowed from Arabic فِعْل مُتَعَدٍّ (fiʕl mutaʕaddin).

def: adj. Of or relating to a verb; derived from a vero, verbal; — (in colloq. Urdū) s.m. A pretender, dissembler; an impostor, a knave.

s.m. = fěʻlī, q.v. the twenty-sixth letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twentieth of the Arabic, and twenty-third of the Persian alphabet). Its sound is that of the English f. It has no exact correspondent in Sanskrit, nor in any of the modern Aryan languages of India; in words introduced into these from the Persian or Arabic, फ ph is generally substituted for fe. As a numeral fe denotes 80.
s.m. (colloq.), Pretence; imposture, knavery.

prep. In, into; during; of, concerning, in respect of; among, with, together with, along with; by; for, on account of, &c.; — each, for each, per (e.g. fī ṣad or ṣadī, 'per cent.'; fī sāl, 'per annum'; fī man, 'per maund'; fī rūpīya, 'each rupee, per rupee,' &c.); — (in Urdū) s.f. Something hidden or behind; something amiss; a defect or flaw; an intrigue (e.g. is bāt meṅ kućh fī hai)
more: 1. In ( bīch ) 2. Per; each. Fī bīgah Per bigah . fī , n. f. 1. Flaw. 2. Intrigue ( sāzish ).

prepos. 1. In, into among, of, to, with, for, by, concerning. 2. Each (or, for each), per; as, في من fī man, Per maund, &c. في البديـﮩ‍ه fi-l-badīha, adv. Readily, quickly, extempore. في الجمله fi-l-jumla, Upon the whole. في الحال fi-l-ḥāl, Instantly, on the spot, immediately, directly, now. في الحقيقت fi-l-ḥaḳīḳat, adv. Really, truly, in effect, in fact, indeed. في الفور fi-l-faur, adv. Now, instantly, in short, immediately, directly. في المثل fi-l-mas̤al, adv. Allegorically. في الناروالسقر fi-n-nāri wa-s-saḳar, In hell flames. في الواقع fi-l-wāḳiﻌ, adv. In fact, indeed, certainly, very true.
subs: adv. Readily, quickly; extempore; without premeditation or deliberation, off-hand; spontaneously; — prima facie
adv. Upon the whole; in brief; in effect
adv. At the present time, now; presently; instantly, immediately, directly, on the spot
adv. Really, truly, in reality, in effect, in fact, indeed
adv. Of a truth; in fact, indeed, really, certainly, very true
In that which; as to what
In that which is after, in what follows; as to the future; henceforth
As to what there is between (us, &c.); between; reciprocal
Between the two parties, inter partes
(-meṅ), To find something wrong or amiss (in); to find fault (with), pick holes (in)
vulg. fīh, In him; in it; in that
In her, or it, or that

ety: inf. n. of فدي 'to give a ransom; to ransom,' &c.
def: s.f. A ransom; redemption; sacrifice; consecration, devotion; devoting oneself (to save another); a thing devoted; — adj. Devoted (to); sacrificed or dying (for)
more: n. فدي gave up, ransomed. Sacrifice; devotion. fidā , adj. 1. Devoted to. 2. Dying for. fidā k. , v. a. To sacrifice; devote; lay at one's feet. fidā h. , v. n. To be sacrificed. Tum par se jān bhī fidā hai .
(Heb. To redeem) s. f. Sacrifice, consecration, redemption, devoting oneʼs self to save another, ransom, exchange, cartel. فداکرنا fidā karnā, To devote, to sacrifice.
subs: v.t. To sacrifice (a thing, or oneself, for), to devote (oneself, or a thing, for, or to, -par, or -ke ūpar, or meṅ)
v.n. To become a ransom (for), be sacrificed (for, -kī), to be devoted (for or to, -par).

ety: rel. n. fr. fidā
def: adj. & s.m. Devoted; offering oneself as a ransom or a victim; — one who sacrifices or devotes himself for (another); one who volunteers or risks his life in any perilous service; — a lover; a thief; a murderer.
more: s. One who willingly hazards life in an act or for any one; a lover, thief, murderer.

ety: prob. contrac. of A. fidāwī, rel. n. fr. fidā, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Devoted; — your devoted servant (much used by inferiors instead of the pronoun 'I').
more: n. m. Your devoted servant; yours obediently.

ety: abst. s. fr. fidwī
def: s.f. Devotedness, devotion; allegiance.

ety: for A. فدية fidyat, fr. فدي
def: s.m. Ransom ( = fidā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فدیه (fidya), from Arabic فِدْيَة (fidya).

ety: for afg̠ān, orig. afgān; prob. fr. Zend gā with prep. aiwi = S. प्र+गै
def: s.m. Cry of pain or distress, wailing, groaning, lamentation, complaint; clamour; — intj. Oh! alas!
more: s. m. f. 1. Lamentation, clamour, complaint. 2. interj. Alas!

ety: rt. of figandan = afgandan; see fikandanī
def: part. adj. Casting, throwing or casting down; overthrowing ( = afgan).

ety: = afgār; prob. Zend aiwi+kāra, rt. kar; S. अभि+कर् (कृ)
def: adj. Wounded, sore, galled; crippled; lame; wounding; afflicting; — afflicted; distracted, confused; — s.m. (?) Soreness of back; a sore on the back (of a beast of burden)
more: adj. 1. Lame, crippled, wounded. 2. Afflicted, confused, distracted. دل فگار dil-figār, Heart-afflicting. فگار دل figār-dil, adj. Wounded at heart, afflicted in mind.
subs: adj. Wounded at heart, heart-sore, afflicted in mind

def: s.f. Wounding; afflicting, affliction (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-figārī).
more: s. f. in compos. Wounding, affliction.

def: (inf. n. of فقد 'to lose,' &c.), s.m. & f. Loss, miss, deprivation, want.

ety: inf. n. of فقه 'to possess knowledge' (esp. of the law
def: s.f. Knowledge, understanding (esp. of the law); — the science of the (Muhammadan) law, jurisprudence; theology.
more: n. f. The Mah. ecclesiastical law. faqīh' , n. m. One well versed in the fiqah , q. v. Fiqah paṛh-kar faqīh ho gaë, magar amal na kīyā .
s. f. Knowledge of religion and law, theology, jurisprudence.

r: fikr, fikr karnā, kī, kā, fikr-mand
ety: v.n. fr. فکر 'to think (upon
def: adj. Thoughtful; anxious; pensive, sorrowful
more: n. m. فکر considered. 1. Reflection ( bichār , 2). 2. Concern; care; solicitude; heed ( chintā ). 1. Apnā fikr āp kar legā He can take care of himself. 2. Yeh tumhāre fikr meṅ phirtā hai . He is plotting mischief against you. 3. Fikr burā, fāqā bhalā, fikr faqīrāṅ khāë . Prov. Better fast than care, 'tis care the beggar kills. (Care will kill a cat). 4. Jiskā fikr uskā zikr . Prov. What's in the heart is on the tongue. 5. Usko sab kī fikr hai . Prov. Saying. God takes care of all. 6. Ek hāth zikr par, dūsrā fikr par . Prov. One hand upon his beads, for bread the other pleads. fikr k. , v. a. 1. To ponder ( bichārnā , 1, 3). Tan sūkhā,kubṛī pīṭh huī, ghoṛe par zīn dharo, bābā! Ab maut-naqārā bāj chukā, chalne kā fikr karo, babā! On old age. Nazir. Your body shrunk, your back is bent, come saddle your steed now, Sir! Death hath his trumpet blown, to march away you need now, Sir! 2. To cont…

s. f. and m. 1. Thought, reflection, consideration, opinion, notion, conceit, imagination, idea, care, concern, counsel, advice. 2. Solicitude, anxiety. فکر مند fikr-mand, adj. Thoughtful, anxious. فکر کرنا fikr karnā, To consider, reflect, think, be thoughtful or anxious, to care.
subs: (-kī), To be constantly thinking (of); to harbour designs (against); to be anxious to injure

adj. & s.m. Without thought, thoughtless; without forethought; without concern or anxiety, unconcerned; free from care; — a thoughtless or heedless person; — be-fikrī, s.f. Thoughtlessness; regardlessness, unconcern; freedom from care.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِکْر (fikr), from Arabic فِكْر (fikr). First attested as Old Hindi फिकरि (phikari).

ety: for A. فقرة fiqrat, v.n. fr. فقر; see faqr
def: s.m. Vertebræ (of the back); — a choice phrase or sentence (as being likened to a vertebra of the back); fine or specious speech or words; — a line; a sentence, a period
more: n. m. فقر broke. 1. A sentence; a period. 2. Deception ( buttā ). 3. ( Anat .) The vertebraë ( mohrah ). fiqrah banānā, yā ghaṛnā , v. a. To pretend; make up a story. fiqrah chalānā, yā denā , v. a. To cheat; deceive ( dam meṅ lānā ). fiqre-bāz , n. m. A cheat ( dam-bāz ). fiqre-bāz , adj. Artful ( dam-bāz ). fiqre-bāzī , n. f. Deception; cheating ( dam-bāzī ). fiqre-bandī , n. m. Construction of a sentence; composition. fiqroṅ meṅ ā jānā , v. n. To be taken in by one's specious speeches. Haṅs-ke kahne lagī yeh woh magrūr, 'Aise fiqroṅ meṅ ā gaī maiṅ zurūr .' Shauq. The vain thing laughed and said, 'Of course, I'm taken in by your glib discourse!'
(from فقر To break, especially the vertebræ) s. m. A line, a sentence. فقره بندي fiḳra-bandī, s. f. Arrangement, eloquence.
subs: vulg. fiqre-bāz, adj. & s.m. Eloquent, glib-tongued, plausible, artful; — an artful fellow, a knave
To make up a fine sentence or speech; to make up or invent a story
s.f. Arrangement or construction of a sentence, composition; eloquence
or fiqra denā, To bring forward, or to present, fine or specious sentences or words, to wheedle or deceive with fine phrases; to deceive, cheat
(kisī-ke), To be deceived or taken in by the specious sentences or speeches (of); to be talked over (by).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِقْرَه (fiqra), from Arabic فِقْرَة (fiqra).

ex: اِس تَرازُو میں بَرابَر ہَیں وہ نیتا ہُوں کِہ فِیل۔ کھاتے ہَیں جَنتا کا حِصَّہ بھی یہ جَنتا کے وَکِیل۔

Wom. 2. Marne kā to dar nahīṅ! kahīṅ farishta ghar na dekh le! Prov. You have not even the fear of death! mind, the destroying angel does not find you out! farishte khāṅ . A strong or powerful man.
