ety: fr. fishāndan = afshāndan = shawāndan, caus. of shudan; rt. Zend shu with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+च्यु (च्यवते)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Scattering, strewing; shedding; spreading, expanding, diffusing; strewed, — scatterer, strewer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ātash-fishān, 'scattering fire').
more: part. (of فشاندن) in compos. Strewing, scattering, shedding, spreading, expanding, strewed, scattered.

ety: perf. part. of fishāndan; see fishān
def: part. Scattered, strewed; shed, spilt; spread, diffused.
more: part. pass. (of فشاندن) Scattered, strewed, spread.

ety: fishān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Scattering, strewing, &c. (used, like fishān, in comp., e.g. jān-fishānī, q.v.).
more: in compos. s. f. Strewing, shedding, scattering, spreading.

ety: inf. n. of فسق 'to quit the right way,' &c
def: s.m. Vicious or immoral conduct; sin, iniquity; impiety; falsehood; immorality; adultery; obscenity; impudence
more: n. m. فسق transgressing. Sin; adultery.
(Heb. To divaricate) s. m. Adultery, obscenity, impudence, iniquity, sin, impiety, falsehood.
subs: s.m. Sin and iniquity; vice and immorality; adultery, &c.

ety: fi = af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+sos = soz? q.v.
def: s.m. &c. = afsos, q.v. s.m. Sorrow, grief, concern; regret; vexation; — intj. Ah! alas! alack! ah me! what a pity!
subs: afsos karnā (-kā, or par), To feel regret, sorrow, &c., to regret; to grieve, sorrow, or sigh (for, or because of), to lament, bemoan; to take to heart; to express sorrow or regret (for); to feel or to express pity (for).

def: s. m. Ribbon, tape. (Portug. fita).

ety: fr. Port. fita
def: s.m. Ribbon; tape.
part. = uftāda, q.v. part. adj. Fallen, lying flat or horizontally; lying waste or untilled (land); poor, wretched, helpless
more: part. pass. (contract. from افتادن) Fallen. See افتاده uftāda.

def: s.f. = uftādagī, q.v.

ety: for فتنة fitnat, quasi-inf. n. of فتن 'to burn'; to try by fire,' &c.
def: s.m. Trial, affliction, calamity, mischief, evil, torment, plague, pest (applied to persons as well as things, e.g. yěh laṛkā bhī baṛā fitna haī); — temptation, seduction; — discord, conflict, cabal, faction, civil war, sedition, revolt, mutiny; perfidy; sin, crime
more: n. m. فتن burned. See balvā . 1. Sedition; faction. 2. Revolt. 3. Evil; pest. fitnah uṭhānā , v. a. To raise a disturbance, sedition, etc. fitnah-angez, fitna-pardāz , adj. See daṅgaī , 2, 3. fitnah-aṅgez, fitnah-pardāz , n. m. See āg-lagāū , 2.
Hin. Wom. adj. Naughty; wicked. Yeh laṛkā bhī baṛā fitnā hai . Wom. gul-i-fitna , n. m. A species of mimosa or acacia bearing strong scented flowers.

(from فتن To try to tempt) s. m. 1. Calamity, mischief. 2. Sedition, mutiny. 3. Perfidy. 4. Sin, evil, pest. 5. Temptation, seduction. فتنه انگيز fitna-angez, A fomenter of disturbances, an incendiary, a mischiefmaker, seditious, turbulent, insurgent, &c. فتنه انگيزي fitna-angez-ī, s. f. Causing disturbances, seditiousness, mischievousness, &c. فتنه جو fitna-jo, Quarrelsome, seditious. فتنه ساز fitna-sāz, Mischiefmaking, mischiefmaker. فتنه سازي fitna-sāz-ī, s. f. The occasioning of disturbance or trouble. فتنه گر fitna-gar, A seditious person, a mischiefmaker.
subs: v.n. To raise a revolt or sedition, &c.
or fitna-pardāz, adj. & s.m. Mischievous, seditious, turbulent, insurgent, &c.; — a mischief-maker, a fomenter of disturbances, a turbulent fellow, an incendiary, &c.
adj. & s.m. Seeking disturbance, seditious; quarrelsome, turbulent; — a quarrelsome or turbulent fellow, &c.
adj. & s.m. Mischief-making, sedition-causing; — mischief-maker, a seditious person, &c.
s.m. A species of mimosa or acacia bearing flowers and having a powerful scent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِتْنَه (fitna), from Arabic فِتْنَة (fitna).

ety: for A. فطنة, inf. n. of فطن
def: s.f. = fat̤ānat, q.v.
s. (dakh.) Rice-milk of a thicker consistency than khīr (Qanoon-e-islam).

ety: v.n. fr. افطر aft̤ara, 'to break a fast,' iv of فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.m. The breaking of a fast; — the festival held on the breaking of the fast of Ramazān ( = īduʼl-fit̤r, q.v.s.v. ʻīd).
s.m. = fit̤rat, q.v.
s.m. Saddle-straps; cords fixed to a saddle for hanging game to.
more: s. m. Cords fastened to a saddle behind, meant for hanging game to; saddle-straps.

ety: for A. فطرة, v.n. fr. فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.f. Creation; production; — the natural constitution, or nature, or form (with which one is created); — nature, constitution; natural disposition or temper; a natural or innate quality or property; idiosyncrasy; — wisdom, sagacity, shrewdness, skill, ingenuity; astuteness, cunning; deceit, trick, artifice, device, plan, scheme, plot, intrigue, machination, stratagem; — (fr. fit̤r, q.v.) the alms of the breaking of the fast of Ramazān
more: n. f. فطر made. 1. Nature; creation. 2. Wisdom ( dānāī ). 3. Artifice; plot. 4. Intrigue; machination. 5. See upāë , 1, 4, 5. fitrat k. , v. a. To scheme; plot, etc. fitrat laṛānā , v. a. To plot and counterplot; to practise tricks or stratagems.

s. f. 1. Wisdom, sagacity, cunning. 2. Nature, creation, form. 3. Deceit, trick. 4. Alms given on the عيد الفطر īﻌdu-l-fit̤r. فطرت لڙانا fit̤rat laṛānā, v. a. (vulg.) To practise tricks or stratagems, to cheat, to shuffle.
subs: v.n. To exercise (one's) skill or ingenuity; to practise artifices or stratagems; to plot and counterplot; to cheat, to shuffle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فطرت (fitrat), from Arabic فِطْرَة (fiṭra, “human nature”).

def: adj. Sagacious, cunning.

ety: rel. n. fr. fit̤rat
def: adj. & s.m. Natural, constitutional; — sagacious, shrewd; cunning, artful, designing, crafty; — a cunning or artful person, &c.
more: adj. 1. Natural. 2. ( fitrat-bāz ) Designing; crafty ( chālāk , 3).

ety: for afzā, rt. & imperat. of afzūdan; Pārsī awazā; rt. Zend śā with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. adj. Increasing, augmenting, enhancing (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jān-fazā, 'life-increasing').
more: part. in compos. (from فزودن) Increasing. See افزا afzā.

ety: fizā, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Increasing, &c. (used, like fazā, in comp.).
air force
wety: Borrowed from Persian فضایی (fazâyi), from Arabic فَضَائِيّ (faḍāʔiyy).

def: s. (dakh.) Hammer.
flight (a trip made by an aircraft)
wety: Borrowed from English flight.

ety: inf. n. of فحش 'to be gross, or lewd, or indecent,' &c.
def: s.m. Gross, foul, immodest, lewd, or obscene, speech or language; grossness, obscenity (in word or deed), ribaldry, bawdry; — adj. Gross, indecent, obscene, &c. (e.g. fǒḥsh bāteṅ, 'foul or indecent language;' fǒḥsh kitāb, 'an obscene book').
more: adj. Obscene; bawdy; indecent. fohsh , n. m. Obscenity; bawdry. fohsh bāteṅ, yā zabān . Obscene language. fohsh bāteṅ baknā, fohsh baknā , v. n. To talk smut or bawdy. fohsh sher . Obscene ballad or verse. fohsh kitāb . An obscene book. fohsh kitāboṅ kā bechnā . Sale of obscene books. fohsh gāliyāṅ , n. f. Indecent abuse. fohsh gīt . A bawdy song.
s. m. Obscenity (in word or deed), bawdry, grossness.
wety: From Arabic فحش (fuḥš).

def: direct plural of فوج (foj, “army”)
vocative plural of فوج (foj, “army”)
oblique plural of فوج (foj, “army”)
to phone, to telephone, to call, to contact via telephone
s.m. = فوطه fot̤a, q.v.

ety: fr. fota = pota = S. पुट
def: s.m. A bag; purse; the scrotum, the testicles; — tax, revenue, collections
more: n. m. 1. The scrotum; testicles. 2. Taxes; revenue; collections. fota bharnā ; Rus. potā bharnā , v. a. To pay tax or revenue. fote chhiṭaknā , v. n. To be ruptured (the testicles). fote-khāna , n. m. A treasury ( khazāna , 1). fote-dār , n. m. Cashier; treasurer. fote-dārī The office of collecting tenant dues.

(Pers. پوته) s. m. 1. A bag. 2. The scrotum, the testicles. 3. Tax, revenue, a purse. فوطه دار fot̤a-dār (vulg. پو تدار) A banker, cash-keeper, cashier, treasurer. فوطه داري fot̤a-dār-ī, s. f. Bankership.
subs: (-meṅ), To pay a tax or revenue
vulg. fot̤e-dār, or fot̤-dār, s.m. Treasurer, banker, cashier, cash-keeper; a collector of tenant-dues
vulg. fot̤e-dārī, s.f. Bankership, treasurership; — the office of a collector of tenant-dues.

def: Frankfurt, Frankfurt-am-Main (the largest city in Hesse, in central Germany); Frankfurt, Frankfurt-an-der-Oder (a sizable town in Brandenburg, in eastern Germany)
wety: Borrowed from English Frankfurt.

def: France (a country located primarily in Western Europe)
wety: Borrowed from English France.

def: s.m. Valerian.

ety: rt. فأد
def: s.m. The heart.
cry, lamentation, wail
wety: Variant of اَفْغَان (afġān), from Classical Persian اَفْغَان (afġān).

ety: v.n. fr. فجل 'to be thick, or flaccid'
def: s.m. A radish, Raphanus sativus; — the heel; — adj. Thick.
more: 1. s. m. A radish. 2. The heel. 3. adj. Thick.

ety: inf. n. of فجر 'to deviate' (from
def: s.m. Wickedness; immorality; adultery; lust.
s.m. pl. (of faqīh), Lawyers; theologians.
s.m. pl. (of faqīr), The poor; religious mendicants, derwishes.

ety: for orig. pūlād
def: s.f. Steel
more: n. m. Steel. faulādī , adj. Made of steel. faulād kā kushtah , n. Calcined steel. faulād kā arq , n. Tincture of steel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فولَاد (fōlād).

ety: rel. n. fr. fūlād
def: adj. Of steel, steel-; — s.f. A pike-staff.
more: 1. adj. Made of steel. 2. s. f. A pikestaff.

def: adj. & s.m. Such a; such and such; a certain (man); — such a man, such a one; so and so; such a thing; — the unmentionable thing or part, penis virilis.
more: (vulg. falān, Heb. from To lie hid) adj. Such a one or thing. ¶ falān or فلاني falānī, s. f. ( met. ) Pudendum muliebre.

def: adj. pron. A certain person or thing; such a one. 1. Shaksh-i-falāṅ . 2. Falāṅ bāt . 3. Kahne lagī, ki falānā ādmī yūṅ kahtā thā. falān , n. m. The organs of generation. falān-marānī , n. f. A woman who prostitutes herself for pleasure, in contradistinction to peṭ marānī , q. v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فلان (fulān), from Arabic فُلَان (fulān).

ety: for A. فلانة fulānat, fem. of fulān
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) = fulān, q.v.; — falānī, s.f. Pudendum muliebre. adj. & s.m. Such a; such and such; a certain (man); — such a man, such a one; so and so; such a thing; — the unmentionable thing or part, penis virilis.
more: (or vulg. falāna ) Such a one, such a, a certain. ¶ falāna, s. m. Penis virilis.

def: s.m. (pl. of fals, q.v.; but used as a sing. also), Small copper coins; — a copper coin, a paisa (Ang. 'pice'); money.
more: n. m. A copper coin; a pice ( paisā ). Yak fallūs, va yak bād-kash, va qadre shīrīnī, va yak surāhī Sirī Krishn ārpan mediham . Kāyaths' play. A satire on the proneness of half-witted Persian and Arabic scholars to foist in learned words with the vernacular Hindī.
s. plur. m. (of فلس) Small coins, a paisā.

def: phone, telephone; phone call, telephone call
ex: میرے بھائی کا فُون آیا ہَے۔
mere bhāī kā fūn āyā hai.
I got a phone call from my brother.
wety: Borrowed from English phone, from telephone, borrowed from French téléphone, from télé- + -phone.

ety: an arabicised form; also bunduq, 'a ball, bullet'; 'a nut' (whence prob. bundūq or bandūq, 'a gun'); old forms are fendak, pendak; prob. akin to S. piṇṇa, or piṇḍaka, 'a ball'
def: s.f. A hazel-nut; — (met.) the stained tips of the fingers or toes (as being likened to hazel-nuts)
more: n. f. 1. The filbert nut. 2. ( Met .) The tips of the fingers or toes stained and resembling filberts.
s. f. The filbert nut; ( met. ) The tips of the fingers or toes stained and resembling those nuts. فندق یند finduḳ-band, Staining the tips of the fingers, &c. to resemble the filbert nut.
subs: s.f. Staining the tips of the fingers, &c. (to resemble the hazel-nut).

def: s.m. pl. (of fann, or fan), Ways, modes, varieties, diversities (rare); — arts, sciences; — arts, tricks, wiles, &c.

ety: prob. = S. पुरु; cf. fag̠-fūr
def: s.m. Name of a king of Kannauj (killed by Alexander the Great), Porus.
more: n. prop. Name of a king of Ḳannauj, killed by Alexander the Great. (Perhaps, Porus).

ety: for A. فرادَ furāda, quasi-plur. of fard, q.v.
def: adj. & adv. Sole, single, separate, solitary, unique; — singly, separately, one by one, one at a time, one after another
more: plur. (of فرد) Alone, single, so litary.

def: (v.n. fr. فرت 'to be sweet,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'Sweet, or thirst-subduing' (water); the river Euphrates.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرات (furāt), from Arabic الْفُرَات (al-furāt), ultimately from Akkadian 𒀀𒇉𒌓𒄒𒉣 (^(ÍD)Purattu).

ety: inf. n. of فرق; see farq
def: s.m. (orig. 'Anything that makes a separation or distinction between truth and falsity'; (hence) the Qǒrʼān.
more: (from فرق as distinguishing truth from falsehood) s. m. The ḳurʼān (Alcoran).

ety: for A. فرقة furqat, v.n. fr. فارق 'to separate oneself from,' &c., iii of فرق; see farq
def: s.f. Separation, disunion; abandonment; absence (of a beloved person); — distinction; distance ( = farq).
more: s. f. Distance, separation, distinction, absence (of lovers, &c.)

ety: for furoḵẖ, by change of خ to غ; see furoz, and furoḵẖta
def: s.m. Illumination, light, brightness, splendour; flame; — glory, fame, honour
more: n. m. Brightness (رونق 2). farog pānā, yā h. , v. n. To get name, honor, etc.
subs: v.n. To get glory, or honour, &c.
v.n. To become famous, or illustrious, &c.

ety: of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; rt. Zend ruć with prep. aiwi or aibi = S. अभि+रुच् (रोचति)
def: Kindled, inflamed; burnt; — shining, radiant ( = afroḵẖta).
more: part. pass. (of فروختن) Burnt, kindled, inflamed.

ety: rt. of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta
def: part. adj. Illumining, enlightening, kindling, inflaming (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-furoz, 'heart-inflaming'); — s.m. Splendour, brilliance, &c.
more: 1. part. act. (of فروختن) in compos. Kindling, inflaming, enlightening. 2. s. Splendour. فروز کرنا furoz karnā, To illuminate.

def: part. (of furoḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta, and furoz), Shining, luminous, resplendent; inflaming.
more: (part. act. of فروختن) adj. Inflaming, shining.

ety: for A. فرصة, v.n. fr. فرص 'to slit, cut, divide,' &c.
def: s.f. A time, opportunity, occasion; freedom (from), leisure; convenience; relief, recovery; respite, reprieve; rest, ease; (local) leave
more: n. f. فرص slit. See فراغت and سوفته 1. Leisure. 2. E. Leave ( chhuṭṭī ). 3. Recovery; relief. fursat chāhnā, yā māṅgnā , E. v. a. To ask leave. fursat denā , v. a. 1. To allow time, occasion, or opportunity for. 2. To give leave. fursat milnā, yā pānā , v. n. 1. To have time or leisure for; to be at leisure. 2. To obtain leave. 3. To be discharged, dismissed. fursat meṅ , adv. At one's leisure or convenience; leisurely. fursat h. , v. n. 1. To be at leisure. 2. To recover from illness. fursat yā mauqe ko hāth se na denā . To lose no opportunity; strike while the iron is hot; to make hay while the sun shines.
(from فرص) s. f. Leisure, freedom, ease, convenience, opportunity, occasion, time, relief, rest.
subs: or furṣat milnā, v.n. To find or get time or leisure (for); to be at leisure; to find or get relief or respite (from, -se); to be dismissed or discharged (from); to obtain leave
(-ko), To grant or allow time or opportunity (for); to afford relief (to), to respite, reprieve; to give or grant leave (to)
adv. At (one's) leisure, or convenience, leisurely
(-ko), To have leisure, be at leisure; to be relieved, to recover (from illness).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فُرْصَت (fursat), borrowed from Arabic فُرْصَة (furṣa). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi فرصت (frṣt).

def: direct plural of فُرْصَت (fursat)
oblique plural of فُرْصَت (fursat)
vocative plural of فُرْصَت (fursat)

s.f. pl. (of فرع farʻ, q.v.), Tops, summits, heads; branches; subdivisions; ramifications; derivatives; — the derivative institutes of the law; fundamental principles or doctrines (of law or religion); — nobles, men of eminence (of a people or party), chiefs (of tribes or families).
more: s. plur. (of فرع) Tops, summits, branches, heads or chiefs of a family.

def: s.f. pl. (of furūʻ), Summits; branches; ramifications ( = furūʻ, q.v.).

ety: rel. n. fr. furūʻ
def: adj. Branch; collateral, indirect; additional; — s.f. Income or revenue other than that from a fixed source, additional income.
s.m. pl. (of farsh, q.v.), Carpets, floor-cloths, &c.
s.m. pl. (of firāsh), Carpets; beds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرش (farš), from Arabic فَرْش (farš, “carpet, rug”).

r: fushurda, fashurda, fushurdan, fashārdan, afshārdan, afshurdan, fashār
ety: perf. part. of fushurdan = fashārdan = afshārdan = afshurdan; see fashār
def: part. Squeezed, pressed, compressed; wrung
more: part. pass. (of فشردن) Squeezed, pressed. فشرده جگر fushurda-jigar, Squeezed heart, afflicted.
subs: adj. Having the heart wrung, afflicted.

ety: = afsūn, q.v. prob. akin to afsāna, q.v.
def: s.m. Enchantment, incantation, fascination, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Enchanting, fascinating, bewitching, &c.; — an enchanter, sorcerer, &c.

def: part. adj. = afsurda, q.v. part. adj. Frozen, frigid, benumbed; withered, faded; dispirited, dejected, low-spirited, melancholy
more: part. pass. (of فسردن) Frozen, withered, dejected. See افسرده afsurda.
subs: adj. Depressed in mind, dispirited, afflicted — afsurda-dil, adj. Fainthearted, low-spirited, dejected.

def: s.f. = afsurdagī, q.v. s.f. Frozenness; frigidity, coldness; numbness; dejection, melancholy, lowness or depression of spirits.

ety: corr fr. the English
def: s.m. A foot (of measure).
wety: Borrowed from English foot.

def: football
wety: From English football.

def: s.f. pl. (of fatḥ, q.v.), Victories, conquests; — gains, pickings, perquisites; income received gratuitously; a gratuitous supply (of sustenance, &c.); abundance, easy circumstances.
more: n. f. Pl. of فتح q. v. Gains; pickings; perquisites.
s. f. plur. (of فتح) 1. Victories. 2. ( met. ) Abundance, easy circumstances, income received gratuitously.
